Earth Ancient Past

Media Explorer

Discover how ancient oceans shaped the world we live in today.

From Ancient seas to Modern life


This collection explores evidence of ocean life, including Appalachian limestone formations and visual overviews of life during the Paleozoic Era.

Appalachian Limestone Story

Limestone in the Appalachian Mountains reveals a hidden ocean past.
Credits: Pexel and Canva

View Transcript

When you look at the Appalachian Mountains, it’s hard to imagine they were once underwater. During the Paleozoic Era, this region was covered by shallow seas filled with marine life. invertebrates like brachiopods and trilobites fossils can be found around rock formations of the Mountain.

These tiny organisms left behind calcium-rich shells that formed limestone over millions of years. When acid touches limestone, it fizzes because of calcium carbonate.

This reaction shows us that the rocks beneath us are records of prehistoric life.

Paleozoic Overview

A visual overview of how life evolved during the Paleozoic Era.
